title: cyperus centralis
intro: Cyperus centralis, also known as papyrus sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the sedge family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and fever, due to its ability to stimulate digestion and reduce inflammation. The health benefits of Cyperus centralis include its potential to alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome, reduce swelling, and exhibit antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, Cyperus centralis is often used in the form of dried roots, leaves, or essential oil, and can be consumed as a decoction, infusion, or applied topically to the affected area.
